pages.full_text:
tickets for the Haydon, Bartok Quartets on Monday evening. The restaurant was the first one which had not allowed us to use access. We are now short of cash, & the banks are closed on Monday.

__Monday 2nd August__
	The campsite 'owner' telephoned her boss, who telephoned someone else, so that we can pay by Travellers cheques. The Casaniouge bank only opens on Wednesday morning.
	We have spent the day swimming & washing clothes, & trying to get things dry after a shower of rain.
	He swam again with the Swantons & left for Conques at 6.0. We looked around the town, had supper & left William, Charles & John in the square, on the stone bench outside the Abbey church. The Benedictine monks were friendly & [?chatted]. We left 2 sleeping bags &
pages.notes: Page begins mid-sentence — continuation from previous spread. 'Haydon' may refer to a performer's surname or could be 'Haydn' (the composer) — left as written. 'Casaniouge' is likely the name of a local bank or place; difficult to read with certainty — could be 'Cassaniouze' (a commune near Conques in Aveyron). The word after 'friendly &' is partially cut off at the bottom of the page and partially illegible — best guess '[?chatted]'. The last line 'We left 2 sleeping bags &' is cut off. No handwritten page number visible at top-left. 'Monday 2nd August' fell on a Monday in 1993, consistent with the nearby spread A_0065 context (France trip, Fri July 23rd = 1993-07-23).

pages.full_text:
several books. [?to] The concert was marvellous in the floodlit nave. The programme was Mozart, Bartok & Schumann. Very beautiful.
	Afterwards we woke Peter up off Simon's lap & went & collect John & Charles, who were both asleep, & William. Simon carried Charles back to the van & strapped him onto the kitchen seat. John was very good & walked back holding Eve's hand & a piggy-back half-way. We drove about 3 miles north from Conques & turned off into a field & all slept in the van [?2.00] John slept on the floor & Eve could just use the loo in the night. There was a fantastic view on both sides from the field.

__Tues [?August]__
	We had breakfast in the van - the boys complained it was cold.
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 132 visible in top-right corner. Page begins mid-sentence continuing from the left page. '[?to]' or '[?No]' appears before 'The concert' — possibly a number or connector word, hard to read. '[?2.00]' appears to be a time (2 a.m.) inserted inline. 'Tues [?August]' — the month word is slightly unclear but consistent with the ongoing August entries; the heading is underlined. The text ends at the bottom of the page mid-entry. 'Peter' appears to be a child sleeping on Simon's lap. Year inferred as 1993 from the Monday 2nd August dating on the left page, consistent with series context.
